label: The Migration Period
pitch:
When The Huns Enter Europe around 375, they set a continent in motion. The Crossing of the Rhine on the last night of 406 sent Vandals, Suebi, and Alans pouring into Gaul; within a generation Geiseric Leads the Vandals into Africa and seized Carthage, and in 455 The Vandals Sack Rome. Attila Invades Gaul and was barely stopped at the Catalaunian Plains, and in 476 Romulus Augustulus Deposed ended the Western Roman Empire.

This is the Völkerwanderung — the great shattering. Out of it came the successor kingdoms that became Europe: the Franks under Clovis, the Burgundians, the Lombards who took Italy in 568, and a Roman world that dissolved into a patchwork of barbarian states even as the Plague of Justinian and the climate disaster of 536 hammered what remained.

spans.label: The Hunnic Trigger
spans.description: The Huns' arrival on the Pontic steppe drove the Goths into the empire and built a short-lived steppe empire that peaked under Attila.

spans.label: The Crossing of the Rhine
spans.description: Vandals, Suebi, and Alans broke across the frozen Rhine and moved through Gaul and Hispania toward North Africa.

spans.label: The Vandal Kingdom of Carthage
spans.description: The Vandals built a naval kingdom in North Africa that controlled the western Mediterranean and sacked Rome in 455.

spans.label: The Fall of the Western Empire
spans.description: A rapid succession of puppet emperors ended in 476 when Odoacer deposed the last Western emperor and ruled Italy as king.

spans.label: The Successor Kingdoms
spans.description: Frankish, Burgundian, Visigothic, and Ostrogothic kingdoms replaced Roman administration across the former Western provinces.

spans.label: The Lombard Conquest of Italy
spans.description: The Lombards invaded a war-ruined Italy and seized much of the peninsula, the last great migration into the West.

events.label: The Huns Enter Europe
events.description: Mounted nomads from the eastern steppe overran the Goths and triggered the chain of migrations that ended the Western Empire.

events.label: Gothic Refugees Flood the Empire
events.description: Tens of thousands of Goths crossed the Danube fleeing the Huns, the first wave of peoples pushed into Roman territory.

events.label: The Crossing of the Rhine
events.description: On the last night of 406 a coalition of Vandals, Suebi, and Alans crossed the frozen Rhine, breaking the western frontier for good.

events.label: Vandals, Suebi, and Alans Enter Gaul
events.description: The Rhine peoples ravaged Gaul for three years before moving on toward the Pyrenees and Hispania.

events.label: Constantine III's Usurpation
events.description: A soldier proclaimed emperor in Britain stripped the island and Gaul of troops, accelerating the western collapse.

events.label: Rome Abandons Britain
events.description: The emperor Honorius told the British cities to look to their own defense, ending four centuries of Roman rule on the island.

events.label: The Burgundian Kingdom on the Rhine
events.description: The Burgundians settled a kingdom on the middle Rhine, later remembered in the Nibelung legends.

events.label: The Vandals Cross into Hispania
events.description: Pushed by Rome and the Visigoths, the Vandals and their allies moved into the Iberian Peninsula.

events.label: Geiseric Leads the Vandals into Africa
events.description: The Vandal king Geiseric ferried his people to North Africa, the empire's richest grain province.

events.label: The Vandals Take Carthage
events.description: Geiseric seized Carthage and built a Mediterranean naval power that throttled Rome's grain and tax base.

events.label: Attila Becomes King of the Huns
events.description: Attila built the Hunnic confederation into the terror of two empires, extracting tribute from Constantinople.

events.label: Attila Invades Gaul
events.description: Attila led a vast army into Gaul, sacking cities until a Roman-Visigothic coalition confronted him.

events.label: Battle of the Catalaunian Plains
events.description: Aetius and the Visigoths halted Attila in one of late antiquity's largest battles, his only major defeat.

events.label: Attila Invades Italy
events.description: Attila ravaged northern Italy before withdrawing after a famous meeting with Pope Leo I.

events.label: Attila Dies; the Hunnic Empire Collapses
events.description: Attila's sudden death and the Battle of Nedao shattered the Hunnic confederation within a year.

events.label: Battle of Nedao
events.description: Subject Germanic peoples destroyed the Huns' army and freed themselves, redrawing the map of central Europe.

events.label: The Vandals Sack Rome
events.description: Geiseric's Vandals plundered Rome for two weeks, the second sack of the city in half a century.

events.label: The Domain of Soissons
events.description: A rump Roman state in northern Gaul under Syagrius outlived the Western Empire by a decade.

events.label: Romulus Augustulus Deposed
events.description: Odoacer deposed the boy-emperor Romulus Augustulus, the conventional end date of the Western Roman Empire.

events.label: Odoacer Rules Italy
events.description: The Germanic commander Odoacer ruled Italy as king while nominally recognizing the Eastern emperor.

events.label: Clovis Unites the Franks
events.description: Clovis I welded the Frankish groups into a single kingdom that would become the dominant power in the West.

events.label: Battle of Soissons
events.description: Clovis destroyed the last Roman domain in Gaul, opening the way to Frankish rule.

events.label: The Baptism of Clovis
events.description: Clovis converted directly to Catholic Christianity, aligning the Franks with the Roman population they ruled.

events.label: The Arian-to-Catholic Conversions
events.description: Over the sixth century the Germanic kingdoms abandoned Arianism for Catholicism, fusing ruler and ruled.

events.label: Belisarius Destroys the Vandal Kingdom
events.description: Justinian's general Belisarius reconquered North Africa in a lightning campaign, ending the Vandal state.

events.label: The Franks Absorb Burgundy and Provence
events.description: The sons of Clovis swallowed the Burgundian kingdom and extended Frankish power to the Mediterranean.

events.label: The Successor-Kingdom Law Codes
events.description: Germanic kings issued written codes such as the Lex Romana Visigothorum to govern their Roman and barbarian subjects.

events.label: The Dust Veil of 536
events.description: A volcanic catastrophe dimmed the sun for over a year, causing crop failure and famine across the post-Roman world.

events.label: The Plague of Justinian
events.description: The first recorded bubonic plague pandemic killed a vast share of the Mediterranean population.

events.label: The Lombards Invade Italy
events.description: The Lombards poured into a Byzantine-ruined Italy, the last of the great Germanic migrations into the West.

events.label: Alboin Leads the Lombards
events.description: King Alboin led the Lombard people from Pannonia into Italy, founding a kingdom centered on Pavia.

events.label: The Post-Roman West Takes Shape
events.description: By the late sixth century the Western provinces had become a settled map of Germanic kingdoms, the foundation of medieval Europe.

events.label: The Collapse of the Grain Fleet
events.description: The Vandal seizure of Africa ended the great state grain shipments that had fed Rome, unravelling the Mediterranean economy.
